news.title: AI Won't Replace Educators. But It is Changing How Students Learn.
news.summary: Terri Taylor examines how AI is reshaping student engagement with learning even as teachers remain essential, arguing that the role of educators shifts toward facilitation, curation, and human connection.

news.title: AI and the futures of education
news.summary: UNESCO frames a global pulse-check on AI in education, publishing divergent perspectives on disruptions, dilemmas, and directions. Builds on UNESCO's ethics recommendation, generative-AI guidance, and twin competency frameworks for teachers and students.

news.title: How AI is giving Northern Ireland teachers time back
news.summary: Google DeepMind piece by Lila Ibrahim on how AI is providing concrete time savings for K-12 teachers in Northern Ireland classrooms, with a focus on lesson preparation and administrative load.

news.title: OpenAI launches free ChatGPT for Teachers workspace for U.S. K-12 educators
news.summary: OpenAI introduced a free, educator-specific ChatGPT workspace for verified U.S. K-12 teachers, running through June 2027, with data security, curriculum tools, and Microsoft 365/Google Drive integration.

news.title: State K-12 AI Policy in 2026: Milestones, Momentum and Missing Links
news.summary: Examines how AI is moving into K-12 classrooms through tutoring and content tools, with state-level policy responses gaining momentum but still missing key guardrails around data, assessment, and equity.

news.title: To teach in the time of ChatGPT is to know pain
news.summary: Reports on the growing burden for instructors who increasingly act as detectives as students offload work to generative AI, reshaping assessment design and classroom dynamics in 2026.

news.title: Call for Entries: The AI for Education Awards 2026 (AI4EDU26)
news.summary: UNESCO IITE opened global K-12 awards recognizing impactful and responsible uses of AI in schools, inviting entries from institutions, NGOs, and individuals. Posted May 18, 2026.
